title: Richly Spiced Kenyan Lamb Stew
description: A spicy and chewy one-pot Kenyan dish featuring lamb, served with a soured cream and chive dip, this recipe is shojin-friendly and can be cooked in a papillote for added flavor.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Place the lamb and sauce in a papillote paper and cook for 30 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, topped with a dollop of soured cream and a sprinkle of chives.
